description_long: This is a small, rectangular barn with a simple, classic gable roof that extends symmetrically on both sides. The exterior is clad in horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The roof is dark and appears to be made of aged wood or possibly asphalt shingles, with a pronounced ridge running along the top. There are no visible doors or windows on any side, suggesting a utilitarian structure, possibly for storage or sheltering equipment. The barn sits directly on the ground with no visible foundation or steps. Its compact size and minimal detailing make it suitable for use in rural or peri-urban settings, such as the edge of a park or a community garden.
description: A small, rectangular barn with a gable roof and horizontal wooden siding, featuring no visible doors or windows. The structure has a rustic, weathered look and a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable end of the barn with horizontal wooden siding and no visible openings.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long, flat expanse of the barn's wall and the overhanging dark roof, with no doors or windows.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a gable end and horizontal siding, also lacking any openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain wall and roof overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: no windows visible on any side
